login-required: You are required to log in with your Wikimedia account to perform checks with the search engine.
logout-message: Logging out will prevent you from making search engine checks.
error-description: An error occurred. If it hasn't been reported (try to check), please file an issue or email me. Include the following information:
search-error-retry: repeat the check without using the search engine
settings-description: This page contains some configurable options for the copyvio detector. Settings are saved as cookies.
settings-background-list: Randomly select from a subset of previous Wikimedia Commons Pictures of the Day that work well as widescreen backgrounds, refreshed daily (default).
settings-background-potd: Use the current Commons Picture of the Day, unfiltered. Certain POTDs may be unsuitable as backgrounds due to their aspect ratio or subject matter.
error-no-search-method: No copyvio search methods were selected. A check can only be made using the search engine, links present in the page, or some combination of these.
error-invalid-oldid: The revision ID $1 is invalid. It should be an integer.
error-no-url: Compare mode requires a URL to be entered. Enter one in the text box below, or choose copyvio search mode to look for content similar to the article elsewhere on the web.
error-no-data: Couldn't find any text in $1. Note: only HTML documents, plain text pages, and PDFs are supported, and content generated by JavaScript or found inside iframes is ignored.
error-timeout: The URL $1 timed out before any data could be retrieved.
error-search-error: An error occurred while using the search engine ($1). Note: there is a daily limit on the number of search queries the tool is allowed to make. You may $2.
error-bad-site: The given site (project=$1, language=$2) doesn't seem to exist. It may also be closed or private. $3
error-revision-not-found: The revision ID couldn't be found: $1.
intro-paragraph-1: This tool attempts to detect $1 in articles. In search mode, it will check for similar content elsewhere on the web using Google, external links present in the text of the page, or Turnitin (via EranBot), depending on which options are selected. In compare mode, the tool will compare the article to a specific webpage without making additional searches, like the Duplication Detector.
intro-paragraph-2: Running a full check can take up to a minute if other websites are slow or if the tool is under heavy use. Please be patient. If you get a timeout, wait a moment and refresh the page.
intro-paragraph-3: Be aware that other websites can copy from Wikipedia, so check the results carefully, especially for older or well-developed articles. Specific websites can be skipped by adding them to the $1.
cached-line: Results cached from $2. Originally generated in $3 seconds.
cached-line-search: Results cached from $2. Originally generated in $3 seconds using {{PLURAL:$4|$4 query|$4 queries}}.
cached-tooltip: To save time (and money), this tool will retain the results of checks for up to 72 hours. This includes the URLs of the checked sources, but neither their content nor the content of the article. Future checks on the same page (assuming it remains unchanged) will not involve additional search queries, but a fresh comparison against the source URL will be made. If the page is modified, a new check will be run.
check-results-time-search: Results generated in $1 seconds using {{PLURAL:$2|$2 query|$2 queries}}.
attribution-warning: This article contains an attribution template: {{$1}}. Please verify that any potential copyvios are not from properly attributed sources.
turnitin-source: $1% of revision text ($2 words) found at $3
urls-hidden: {{PLURAL:$1|$1 URL with lower similarity hidden.|$1 URLs with lower similarity hidden.}}
search-ended-early: The search ended early because a match was found with high similarity. $1
